name: Old South Strawberry Shortcake
recipeIngredient: Martha White All-Purpose Flour, sifted (2 1/4 cups)
Butter, cold and cut into pieces (3/4 cup or 1 1/2 sticks)
Strawberries, hulled and sliced (1 1/2 quarts), sweetened to taste
Whipping cream (8 ounces), whipped with 1 tablespoon sugar
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, stir together flour and salt.
2. Cut butter into the flour using a pastry blender or two knives until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Sprinkle water a little at a time over the flour mixture, stirring with a fork until dough forms a ball.
4. Roll out the dough on a lightly floured board or pastry cloth to about 1/4-inch thickness.
5. Cut into sixteen 3 1/2-inch circles using a round cutter.
6. Place the circles on an ungreased baking sheet.
7. Sprinkle sugar over each circle and prick with a fork.
8. Chill thoroughly in the refrigerator.
9. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit (or 190 degrees Celsius).
10. Bake for 22 to 24 minutes, or until the pastry begins to brown.
11. Serve within a few hours or freeze.
12. To serve, place a pastry round on eight individual serving plates; top rounds with half the strawberries.
13. Place another pastry round on top of the strawberries.
14. Top with remaining strawberries and whipped cream.
